items.summary: Top players, including world number ones on the men's and women's tour, criticise the increased French Open prize money pool as not being enough amidst other unresolved welfare demands.

items.title: Separated couples forced to keep living together amid housing crisis
items.summary: Experts say more separated couples are continuing to stay under the same roof because housing is too expensive or scarce.

items.title: High Court to consider who owns rights to abattoir footage captured by activists' secret cameras
items.summary: A goat abattoir, a group of animal rights activists and the copyright act will be the focus today in a High Court challenge over who owns the rights to footage captured by secret cameras.

items.title: PM brushes aside questions over broken promise on negative gearing — as it happened
items.summary: Anthony Albanese has brushed aside questions on if the government has broken a promise to not touch negative gearing in the upcoming budget. Take a look back at the day's coverage below.

items.title: WA government preparing to abandon its emissions reduction targets, documents reveal
items.summary: The WA government is preparing to walk away from its own greenhouse gas emissions reduction targets and dramatically reshape how the state addresses climate change, the ABC can reveal.

items.title: Pharmacists turn patients away for home medicine reviews due to funding caps
items.summary: Pharmacists say government funding caps limiting the number of patients they can see in their homes for medication reviews means many are missing out on life-changing help.

items.title: Campaign calls for a NSW government-run domestic violence body to provide services, peak body reacts
items.summary: NSW's peak body for domestic violence has described a campaign calling for a government-run body to solely provide services as a "huge shift backwards".

items.title: Anglican bishop ordered to resign over relationship with priest appealing decision
items.summary: One of South Australia's most senior Anglicans is stripped of his position over an undisclosed relationship with a priest he ordained, in a case described by the church as "a gross ethical failure".

items.title: Supermarkets urged to absorb more price increases from Iran conflict
items.summary: As the price of food and agricultural production goes up during the Iran conflict, supermarkets are being urged to absorb more of the cost.

items.title: Push for free school lunch program to be adopted nationally
items.summary: Tasmania is "leading the way" with its largely state government-funded school lunch program. Now advocates are calling for a national rollout of the scheme, but warn parents may need to chip in.

items.title: Early voting opens in Stafford by-election with three main issues to sway votes in Queensland state seat
items.summary: Residents in the north Brisbane seat of Stafford are set to cast their votes in a by-election dominated by cost-of-living concerns.

items.title: Queensland trains are running reduced services from today. Here's what you need to know
items.summary: Commuters have been warned to expect "more crowded" trains as a reduced timetable begins, with no end in sight.

items.title: What Jarome Luai's shock NRL move could mean for Australian and New Zealand rugby union
items.summary: The NRL's expansion has ramifications for more than the existing 17 clubs, with Australian and New Zealand rugby talent also in the crosshairs.

items.title: Hung Vuong Stadium construction begins as part of Vietnam transformation plan
items.summary: The country announced more than 500 infrastructure projects last year alone, totalling $280 billion, as it pushes ahead with an ambitious plan to revolutionise its economy.

items.title: Teen with disability sues Australian government over treatment during Vanuatu quake
items.summary: A teenager with multiple disabilities stranded for days in Vanuatu's capital city after a deadly 2024 earthquake is taking the Australian government to the federal court for disability discrimination.

items.title: RBA's decision on interest rates a choice between two evils: recession or inflation
items.summary: If there's any certainty about the RBA's interest rate verdict, it's that it will be a split decision.

items.title: Dialysis units across Australia and New Zealand forced to ration lifesaving care
items.summary: Dialysis units across Australia and New Zealand are being forced to ration lifesaving care for patients with kidney failure, according to a major new report which has warned of a "significant threat" to patient safety.

items.title: The NDIS rorts playbook that's popping up around Australia
items.summary: Insiders and participants say the NDIS is being targeted in similar ways around the country, including billing for services not provided, overcharging and in some cases "kidnapping" of vulnerable clients in order to park them in expensive supported accommodation and drain their plans.

items.title: Labor locks in tax reform trio: capital gains, negative gearing and trusts
items.summary: The government is still finalising the details of its budget, but several sources say three tax policies that featured in Labor's 2019 election campaign will be revived and rethought.

items.title: Met Gala quiz: Who Am I? Guess who wore these outfits to fashion's best party
items.summary: From Ariana to Zendaya, we're testing your knowledge of some of the most exceptional and extreme fashion on show at recent Met Galas.

items.title: Two killed by car driving into pedestrians in German city of Leipzig
items.summary: Authorities say two people are dead after a car slammed into a crowd of people in the eastern German city.

items.title: Hegseth says ceasefire 'is not over', with US 'not looking for fight'
items.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the ceasefire with Iran is still in effect, despite the US and Iran launching attacks in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday. Read how developments on Tuesday April 5 unfolded.

items.title: Cruise ship with suspected hantavirus outbreak banned from docking in Cape Verde
items.summary: Three people have died and three people are ill after a suspected outbreak of the rodent-borne virus in the Atlantic Ocean.

items.title: Electric vehicle tax discount to be wound back
items.summary: A popular tax discount meaning people leasing electric vehicles did not have to pay Fringe Benefits Tax will be wound back — although in the long term, more EV owners will be able to pay a reduced tax rate.

items.title: Super Netball round-up: A 'wishy washy' approach to Pride matches dulls the message
items.summary: Several Super Netball clubs will host their own Pride matches this season, but without an official round to anchor them all a former player and member of the LGBTIQA+ community argues the message may be lost.

items.title: Brisbane Lions player Koby Evans under investigation for alleged homophobic slur in VFL
items.summary: The AFL faces another homophobic slur case, with a Brisbane Lions player alleged to have used a slur in a VFL game against Coburg.

items.title: Treasurer gives reality check on Anthony Albanese's hopes for universal child care legacy
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has previously committed to delivering universal child care, but Treasurer Jim Chalmers has highlighted concerns about the cost.

items.title: AFL umpire Nick Foot's betting agency role over after tribunal case
items.summary: Betting agency Sportsbet announces it will no longer feature current sports administrators or officials in their programming "following feedback".

items.title: Police unions demand answers over taser security flaw that exposes location of officers
items.summary: Police associations and unions demand urgent action over revelations that a security flaw allows officers to be tracked by criminals with a phone or laptop.

items.title: AFL commentary icon Dennis Cometti farewelled at state memorial service in Perth
items.summary: A host of high-profile Australians gather at Perth Stadium to pay tribute to one of the nation's most well-known sports broadcasters, Dennis Cometti.

items.title: Three Tasmanian police employees charged over separate incidents

items.title: Australia and Japan sign agreements on energy, defence and critical minerals
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and his Japanese counterpart Takaichi Sanae agreed to bolster cooperation on energy, defence and critical minerals, while warning against economic coercion.

items.title: Authorities issue warning for bushfire at Herdsman Lake in suburban Perth
items.summary: Authorities issue a warning for possible ember flare-ups as an unseasonal bushfire burns at Herdsman Lake in suburban Perth.

items.title: SA public servants asked to remove word 'harmful' from communications about SA algal bloom, FOI shows
items.summary: Emails released under FOI laws and obtained by the SA Liberal Party show government workers were removing the word "harmful" from communications about the algal bloom in SA waters, with one team leader labelling it "bizarre".

items.title: Shock after baby dies, another hospitalised after birth in Wagga Wagga homeless camp
items.summary: A women's health advocate says the death of a baby during childbirth at a homeless camp on the Murrumbidgee River is a "terrible day" for the Wagga Wagga community.

items.title: Brisbane Broncos reveal timeline of Deine Mariner's leg injury that led to urgent surgery
items.summary: Brisbane Broncos's chief medical officer releases the timeline of Deine Mariner's serious leg injury against the Roosters, leading to "limb-saving surgery" at Sydney's Royal Prince Alfred Hospital.

items.title: Four easy dishes with rice as the star
items.summary: Rice doesn't need to play second fiddle. The cheap and simple pantry staple can hold up a whole meal.

items.title: WA Police reveal details of absurd Triple Zero calls and issue warning over abuse of service
items.summary: Triple Zero call centres are seeing an increase in "absurd" calls, ranging from lost television remote controls to ducks crossing the road.

items.title: Phillip Island residents take on government over 'incorrect' land tax bills
items.summary: Residents on Phillip Island, where building is limited because of inundation and erosion risk, are battling to have their property valuations reduced.

items.title: Dolphins prop Daniel Saifiti retires from NRL due to ongoing shoulder problems
items.summary: Daniel Saifiti's stellar career with the Dolphins, Newcastle, NSW, and Fiji comes to a premature end due to injury.

items.title: Have your say: What would you like to see in the 2026 federal budget?
items.summary: We want to know what issues matter to you ahead of the federal budget. Have your say.

items.title: 400 swimmers compete in Lake Argyle Swim despite crocodile bite a day earlier
items.summary: Four people were bitten by a suspected freshwater crocodile just a day before a sold-out swimming competition was run in the same far-north Western Australia lake.

items.title: $1m reward offered over cold case disappearance of Marion Barter
items.summary: Marion Barter has not been seen since 1997. Her daughter is determined to find out what happened to her mother, who she believes may have found herself "in a situation she did not fully control".

items.title: Socceroos midfielder Aiden O'Neill injures ankle ahead of World Cup, as Harry Souttar returns
items.summary: US-based Socceroos midfielder Aiden O'Neill suffers an ankle injury less than six weeks out from the start of the FIFA World Cup in North America.

items.title: Tim Roth battles Radha Mitchell in Australian action movie Seven Snipers
items.summary: Shooting in snake-and-spider-infested grass on a Queensland farm, Seven Snipers spooked its stars.

items.title: Traffic chaos at Mt Field National Park with visitors lining up to see the turning of the fagus
items.summary: Nature lovers who went to see the autumn colours of the fagus at Tasmania's Mt Field National Park on Saturday say there were no car spaces and nowhere to turn around.

items.title: Police may deploy excavator in search for missing Lake Macquarie teenager Gordana Kotevski
items.summary: Gordana Kotevski was last seen in 1994 when witnesses saw the 16-year-old being forced into a vehicle by at least two people near Newcastle.

items.title: WA government to ban no-grounds evictions amid state's rental housing shortage
items.summary: Under the proposed laws, landlords will no longer be allowed to terminate a tenant's lease without giving a reason.

items.title: Significant shortage of workers to help homeless in Victoria, report finds
items.summary: Victoria needs almost five times as many outreach field workers to support people who are sleeping rough, according to a study by the Council to Homeless Persons.

items.title: Queensland ministers clarify relationship timeline amid integrity questions
items.summary: State ministers Tim Mander and Amanda Camm have clarified the timeline of their relationship after concerns were raised that it was not properly declared.

items.title: Daria Kasatkina wins Catalonia Open to claim first title as an Australian citizen
items.summary: Fighting back from a set down, Daria Kasatkina wins the Catalonia Open final in Spain.

items.title: Uncertainty for Albany businesses as ATCO Australia moves customers to bottled gas
items.summary: ATCO Australia tells 8,000 customers they will need to find new energy sources or switch to bottled gas as it prepares to decommission its Great Southern gas network in WA.

items.title: Healing ceremonies across Northern Territory grieve death of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby
items.summary: The Northern Territory is continuing to grieve the short life of a five-year-old girl today with crowds in Alice Springs to pay tribute by donning pink, her favourite colour.

items.title: Australian rugby sevens player Teagan Levi joins NRLW's Gold Coast Titans
items.summary: The Gold Coast Titans are celebrating a massive signing as they continue preparations for the 2026 NRLW season.

items.title: Prahran bar targeted during night of multiple suspicious fires across Melbourne
items.summary: Police are investigating multiple suspicious fires including an attack on Electric Bar in Prahran, a suspicious house fire in Caulfield North and a fire at a Tullamarine kebab shop.

items.title: Agriculture sector sees 'silver lining' in global disruptions as interest in farming surges
items.summary: The agriculture sector says an increase in attention has been a "silver lining" amid supply chain pressures.

items.title: Pat Cummins endures horror IPL over as Sunrisers fall to Knight Riders in seven-wicket loss
items.summary: Despite taking a wicket, Pat Cummins bowls an over he will prefer to forget, as Sunrisers Hyderabad's five-match winning streak comes to an end.

items.title: Parents of teen who died after sudden cardiac arrest want to bring heart screening to young athletes
items.summary: Two Canberra parents rocked by the sudden death of their teenage son are trying to help him leave a life-saving legacy.

items.title: As voting opens in the ABC Classic 100: Greatest of All Time, we explore how classical music is thriving
items.summary: As Australians vote for the greatest classical music of all time, we look at who is listening, how classical music is evolving, and how it fills the world around us.

items.title: Residents say removal of sperm whale at Bellambi put them at risk of shark attack
items.summary: Swimmers and surfers say they were given no warning before a 25-tonne whale carcass was towed past more than a dozen South Coast beaches, attracting sharks in its wake.

items.title: Coastal council pushes for levy on fossil fuel giants to fund disaster recovery
items.summary: Councillors in a Victorian shire battling coastal erosion say fossil fuel giants should help pay for damage caused by climate-related environmental disasters.

items.title: Sam Kerr becomes Chelsea's all-time leading WSL scorer in 3-1 win over Leicester
items.summary: Sam Kerr creates history in Chelsea's 3-1 win at Leicester, as the Blues secure a berth in next season's Champions League.

items.title: Albanese heralds 'deep friendship' with Japan following Takaichi meeting — as it happened
items.summary: Australia and Japan sign agreements on critical minerals, energy security and defence cooperation following a meeting between Anthony Albanese and Takaichi Sanae.

items.title: Cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz reports being attacked as peace negotiations continue
items.summary: A cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz says it has been attacked by multiple small craft, marking at least two dozen attacks in and around the strait since the Iran war began.

items.title: E-bike sales surge 300 per cent in Darwin as more people ditch cars and costly fuel
items.summary: Bike shops in Darwin are experiencing soaring demand as more people turn to one of the oldest and simplest alternatives to cars amid skyrocketing fuel prices, but advocates say the federal government is still prioritising drivers.

items.title: Ex-Man United manager Alex Ferguson hospitalised after feeling unwell at Premier League match
items.summary: Sir Alex Ferguson is sent to hospital after feeling unwell ahead of his former club's Premier League match against Liverpool.

items.title: How people power is helping to restore bus routes across Sydney
items.summary: Communities fighting to have their local bus or ferry routes restored should campaign hard and "stick it out for the long term", according to these residents in Pyrmont.
